title: pseudognaphalium glanduliferum
intro: Pseudognaphalium glanduliferum, also known as Desert cudweed or Desert ragwort, is a flowering plant species native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, derived from its essential oils and flavonoids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and potentially aiding in wound healing, due to its antioxidant and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of Pseudognaphalium glanduliferum are often made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
